mission.title: Install and Use Score for Kubernetes Workload Deployment
mission.description: Score is a developer-centric, platform-agnostic workload specification. The score-k8s CLI translates Score YAML into Kubernetes manifests, letting you define workloads once and deploy across environments. Score is a client-side CLI tool — it does not run inside your cluster.

steps.title: Install the score-k8s CLI
steps.description:
Install score-k8s using one of these methods:

**Homebrew (macOS/Linux):**
```bash
brew install score-spec/tap/score-k8s
```

**Go install:**
```bash
go install -v github.com/score-spec/score-k8s/cmd/score-k8s@latest
```

**Binary download:**
```bash
# Download from https://github.com/score-spec/score-k8s/releases
# Example for Linux amd64:
wget https://github.com/score-spec/score-k8s/releases/download/0.10.1/score-k8s_0.10.1_linux_amd64.tar.gz
tar xzf score-k8s_0.10.1_linux_amd64.tar.gz
sudo mv score-k8s /usr/local/bin/
```

Verify the installation:
```bash
score-k8s version
```

steps.title: Initialize a Score project
steps.description:
Initialize a new Score project in your working directory:
```bash
score-k8s init
```
This creates a `score.yaml` template that describes your workload in a platform-agnostic way.

steps.title: Create a Score workload specification
steps.description:
Edit `score.yaml` to define your workload. Ports are defined under `service`, not under `containers`:
```yaml
apiVersion: score.dev/v1b1
metadata:
  name: my-workload
containers:
  main:
    image: nginx:1.25
service:
  ports:
    http:
      port: 80
      protocol: TCP
```

steps.title: Generate Kubernetes manifests and deploy
steps.description:
Generate manifests from your Score file and apply them:
```bash
score-k8s generate score.yaml
kubectl apply -f manifests.yaml
```
The `generate` command writes output to `manifests.yaml` by default. To pipe directly:
```bash
score-k8s generate score.yaml -o - | kubectl apply -f -
```

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
Check that your workload was deployed:
```bash
kubectl get pods
kubectl get deployments
kubectl describe deployment my-workload
```

resolution.summary: The score-k8s CLI is installed locally and can translate Score workload specifications into Kubernetes manifests. Score runs entirely client-side — no cluster-side components are installed.

uninstall.title: Remove deployed workloads
uninstall.description:
Delete the resources created from Score:
```bash
kubectl delete -f manifests.yaml
```
Note: resource cleanup in score-k8s is not fully implemented yet. Verify all resources are removed:
```bash
kubectl get all | grep my-workload
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Score CLI
uninstall.description:
Remove the CLI binary:
```bash
# If installed via Homebrew:
brew uninstall score-k8s

# If installed manually:
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/score-k8s
```

upgrade.title: Update the Score CLI
upgrade.description:
Upgrade to the latest version:
```bash
# Homebrew:
brew upgrade score-k8s

# Go install:
go install -v github.com/score-spec/score-k8s/cmd/score-k8s@latest

# Manual: download the latest release from
# https://github.com/score-spec/score-k8s/releases
```

troubleshooting.title: Generated pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This is an issue with the workload definition, not Score itself. Check the pod logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name>
kubectl describe pod <pod-name>
```
Verify your `score.yaml` references a valid container image and correct port configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Generate command fails with validation error
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure your `score.yaml` follows the v1b1 spec. Common mistakes:
- Ports must be under `service.ports`, not `containers.*.ports`
- `apiVersion` must be `score.dev/v1b1`
- Container names must be alphanumeric

Re-initialize to get a valid template:
```bash
score-k8s init --overwrite
```

troubleshooting.title: Binary not found after install
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure the binary is on your PATH:
```bash
which score-k8s
echo $PATH
```
If installed via `go install`, ensure `$GOPATH/bin` (or `$HOME/go/bin`) is in your PATH.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster with kubectl configured. For installing the CLI: Homebrew, Go 1.22+, or wget/curl for binary download.
